disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE SCAFFOLDING for the Aleron design loop. Every clinical number (prevalence, threshold, detector sensitivity, base_catch) is invented to fix the closed-world findings structure, sourced to a real evidence synthesis and kept internally consistent -- NOT validated for clinical use. A FINDING is anything true about a patient that, if detected, enables a QALY-positive action vs the frozen current-management baseline. kind=continuous: graded by a true risk, 'present' above prevalence_or_threshold, per-patient delta scales with risk. kind=rare: binary, individually rare, importance-sampled at population prevalence. detectors map a pricing-catalog test_id to the probability a design buying that test detects the finding. base_catch = probability current management already detects+acts. enables lists the action_library ids this finding unlocks. Genuinely thin-evidence items are kept (never dropped) and flagged in note. Detector test_ids reference /system-design/design-loop/pricing/pricing.json by id; pricing_gaps lists real tests that are NOT yet in the catalog (with proposed CPT + price) and that currently must fall back to a present-but-suboptimal detector.

findings.note: Core lipid-driven 10-yr ASCVD risk (USPSTF 2022 treat threshold >=0.075 selective / >=0.10 recommend). CAC is the strongest single reclassifier; ApoB > LDL-C for discordant/residual risk. Standard care catches most high-risk primary-prevention patients but misses discordant-ApoB / CAC-positive-low-LDL / undertreated intermediates. BP component split into bp_driven_risk so the antihypertensive action has a clean detector.

Threshold = sustained SBP >=130-140 mmHg; true BP-driven ASCVD risk graded (BPLTTC). Kept distinct from cvd_risk_ascvd because the action (antihypertensives) and effect size are separate. Ambulatory BP catches masked/white-coat HTN that office BP misses. BOTH detectors are pricing_gaps (office_bp, ambulatory_bp) -- this finding false-zeros unless they are added to the catalog.
Residual inflammatory risk: hs-CRP >=2 mg/L on optimized lipid lowering (mostly secondary prevention); ~30-40% of statin-treated. Standard care rarely measures or acts. Threshold expressed in mg/L.

Threshold = Lp(a) >=125 nmol/L (~50 mg/dL); ~20% of population. Genetically determined, lifelong, independent causal risk (HR ~1.3-1.5). Once-in-a-lifetime test. Proven action is risk-reclassification -> intensify LDL/BP control; direct Lp(a)-lowering (lpa_targeted_lowering) is WEAK/placeholder pending outcomes trials.
Threshold = TG 150-499 mg/dL on statin with established ASCVD or diabetes+riskfactor (REDUCE-IT phenotype). Action = icosapent ethyl; atrial-fibrillation harm is a real QALY debit.

Heterozygous FH ~1 in 250-300; monogenic autosomal dominant, 3-4x CAD risk, premature CAD ~10 yr early. lipid_panel gives partial phenotypic detection (LDL >190); genetic confirmation enables cascade screening of relatives (large external value). fh_gene_panel is a pricing_gap (cheap targeted LDLR/APOB/PCSK9/LDLRAP1 panel); WES/WGS detect FH but at high cost. cad_prs is polygenic only and misses monogenic. hereditary_cancer_panel does NOT cover FH genes -- excluded as a detector.

HCM ~1 in 500 (actionable subset lower); hypertrophic/dilated/arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathies. Imaging (cardiac_mri/echo_tte) detects phenotype; genetics enables presymptomatic + cascade. Actionable: risk-stratified primary-prevention ICD, activity counseling, cascade screening. Genotype-driven primary-ICD evidence is moderate.

Long-QT / Brugada / CPVT combined ~1 in 1,000-2,000; channelopathies causing SCD in structurally normal hearts. resting_ecg is the cheap first-line phenotypic detector for LQTS but is a pricing_gap; WES/WGS detect genotype. Actionable: beta-blockers (LQTS/CPVT), activity restriction, ICD in high-risk, cascade screening. Evidence moderate, rare and high-value.

LIFESTYLE/behavioral exposure. Binary, intake-detected active tobacco use; prevalence ~0.12 (current cigarette smoking, recent NHIS). smoking_status_intake ($0 pricing_gap) captures it near-universally; cotinine (CPT 82505) is the present-catalog objective-confirmation detector so this finding does NOT false-zero. base_catch 0.38: status is recorded almost everywhere but effective STRUCTURED cessation (counseling + pharmacotherapy) is markedly under-delivered. Credited value (RARE_PENETRANCE 0.25 in generate_cohort.py) is the smoking-attributable death probability OUTSIDE the cardiovascular fraction already booked under cvd_risk_ascvd/bp_driven_risk -- lung cancer + COPD + non-CVD cancers + residual CV. Prevalence: https://www.cdc.gov/tobacco/php/data-statistics/adult-data-cigarettes/index.html

LIFESTYLE/behavioral exposure. Hazardous/heavy alcohol use (AUDIT-C positive). Prevalence ~0.09 (actionable heavy/hazardous band). kind=rare by analogy to hearing_loss_actionable (binary, non-genetic, intake-detected), NOT rare in the 1-in-thousands sense. Sole detector auditc_intake is a $0 pricing_gap, so this finding FALSE-ZEROS against the present catalog (like bp_driven_risk) until an intake screen is catalogued. base_catch 0.20: USPSTF Grade B but screening + brief intervention chronically under-delivered. Credited value (RARE_PENETRANCE 0.05) is the non-ASCVD/BP slice only -- atrial fibrillation, hemorrhagic stroke, alcohol-associated liver disease, alcohol-attributable cancers; deliberately conservative to avoid double-count vs the CVD and cancer-screening findings.

Threshold = UACR >=30 mg/g (KDIGO A2); ~9-10% US adults, risk graded by UACR x eGFR. Albuminuria is the modifiable axis with the largest treatment effect; only ~18-35% of at-risk patients ever get a UACR (drives low base_catch). Detector needs albumin + creatinine for ratio; eGFR alone misses normoalbuminuric staging.

Threshold = eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m2 sustained (G3a+); ~6% US adults, 34% of >=65y. Routine CMP creatinine catches it moderately; cystatin C reclassifies ~1/6 borderline G3a and is underused. Confirm sustained over 3 months.

Threshold = eGFR decline >5 mL/min/1.73m2/yr (rapid loss); subset of CKD heading to kidney failure, where interventions buy the most QALYs. Captures the slope not the level; needs >=2 timepoints (serial testing).

Hypertensive kidney disease, ~2nd leading ESRD cause; largely overlaps ckd_albuminuric. BP measurement widely done (high base_catch) but BP-to-renal-target intensification is the gap. office_bp is a pricing_gap (assumed captured at intake at zero marginal cost); uacr/creatinine are present catalog fallbacks.

ADPKD ~1/400-1/1000. abdominal_us (76700) is a present imaging proxy detecting cysts in adults >40y (lower if young); pre-cyst/pre-symptomatic detection needs genetics. kidney_gene_panel (dedicated PKD1/PKD2) is a pricing_gap; WES/WGS detect it but at high cost. Early detection enables early tolvaptan.

Alport (COL4A3/4/5) ~1/5000; X-linked + AR/AD. Presents with microscopic hematuria + albuminuria, often missed until proteinuria. Genetic dx enables early RAASi (delays ESRD ~13-18 yr). kidney_gene_panel and urinalysis_dipstick (for hematuria) are pricing_gaps; WES/WGS are present fallbacks.

Two APOL1 high-risk alleles: ~13% of Black Americans; weighted ~0.5% across a general cohort, far higher in African-ancestry strata. Strong genetic risk for FSGS/proteinuric CKD. apol1_genotyping (cheap 2-variant assay) is a pricing_gap; only WES/WGS detect it in the catalog (vastly over-priced). Targeted therapy (inaxaplin) evidence currently weak pending AMPLITUDE Phase 3.

UMOD/ADTKD, congenital CAKUT, atypical-HUS complement, cystinosis collectively a minority of unexplained CKD (~10-20% of unexplained/early CKD is monogenic on broad sequencing, Groopman NEJM 2019). Heterogeneous; weak/moderate action evidence, wide uncertainty. kidney_gene_panel is a pricing_gap; WES/WGS present.

Threshold = 5-yr T2DM risk >~0.10 (HbA1c 5.7-6.4 / FPG 100-125 / 2h-OGTT 140-199); ~38% of US adults prediabetic. Gateway finding: detection enables DPP/metformin prevention; delta scales with risk. USPSTF grade-B screen at 35-70 + BMI>=25 is standard care but prediabetes is under-acted. OGTT most sensitive (catches isolated post-load).

Threshold = HbA1c >=6.5% or FPG >=126; ~4.5% of US adults undiagnosed. Once detected, opens glycemic control + cardiorenal-protective drug actions. The MACE/HF deltas of the GLP1/SGLT2 actions overlap CVD/CKD home domains -- credit the drug-specific delta here, keep ASCVD-risk and kidney-failure stock in their home domains to avoid double-count.

Threshold = HOMA-IR upper tertile / metabolic-syndrome physiology; overlaps prediabetes/adiposity. WEAK as an independent actionable target beyond what prediabetes/adiposity capture -- flag wide uncertainty; value is mostly as an earlier detector of prediabetes, not a separate intervention. Enables the lifestyle action only (no IR-specific drug on a real outcome passes the validity gate).

Threshold = BMI >=30 (or >=27 with comorbidity); ~40% US adult obesity. BMI/waist are free exam measures (anthropometric_bmi is a zero-cost pricing_gap line, matching the office_bp convention); leptin/dexa are weak adjuncts. Drives incretin/surgery actions; kept distinct from prediabetes so a non-dysglycemic obese patient still earns the weight-driven cardiometabolic action.

Threshold = established T2DM with HbA1c above individualized target (~7%). In-diabetes glycemic-control finding feeding the intensive-control microvascular action. Glycemic control is core standard care so incremental value over baseline is modest. cgm_glycemic_mgmt is borderline validity-gate (benefit runs through HbA1c surrogate) -- flagged weak.

Threshold = T2DM + UACR >=30 mg/g or eGFR decline (cardiorenal-high-risk subset). Flags patients for SGLT2/GLP-1 cardiorenal-protective therapy. Overlaps the CKD domain's ckd_albuminuric -- assign the diabetes-specific cardiorenal drug delta here, keep kidney-failure progression stock in CKD to avoid double-count.

HNF1A/HNF4A MODY ~0.001 of adults (MODY 1-6% of diabetes, ~50-80% misdiagnosed as T1/T2). Actionable: switch insulin -> low-dose sulfonylurea (often dramatic, decades of avoided insulin). mody_gene_panel is a pricing_gap; c_peptide + negative islet-antibodies give phenotypic triage; WES/WGS detect but at high cost.

GCK-MODY ~0.0008; mild stable fasting hyperglycemia, typically over-treated as T2DM. Actionable: deprescribe -- no treatment needed (except pregnancy); avoids lifelong unnecessary drugs/monitoring. mody_gene_panel is a pricing_gap; WES/WGS detect.

KCNJ11/ABCC8 KATP-channel neonatal diabetes ~1/100,000 births (~0.00001 in adult cohort); ~40% sulfonylurea-responsive. Actionable: insulin -> high-dose sulfonylurea, major glycemic + neurodevelopmental benefit. STRONG effect, vanishingly rare -- flag wide uncertainty on cohort-level contribution. mody_gene_panel (KATP genes) is a pricing_gap; WES/WGS detect.

Familial partial lipodystrophy / severe insulin resistance (LMNA, PPARG, insulin-receptor) ~0.0002. Actionable: metreleptin for leptin-deficient lipodystrophy; very rare, evidence moderate/weak -- flag wide uncertainty. leptin (low in lipodystrophy) is a present phenotypic clue; a dedicated lipodystrophy/SIR gene panel is a pricing_gap folded into mody_gene_panel; WES detects.

Preclinical screen-detectable CRC ~4/1000 avg-risk colonoscopy CDR (advanced-adenoma precursor folded into delta). Organized CRC screening exists; FIT/colonoscopy widely used. Colonoscopy uniquely REMOVES the precursor (prevention, not just detection).

Population-mean preclinical screen-detectable ~1.5/1000 (~7/1000 within heavy-smoker screen-eligible stratum, weighted down). Screening confined to 50-80yo >=20 pack-yr; real-world uptake low (~5-15%), so most catch is incremental.
Preclinical screen-detectable invasive+DCIS ~5/1000 (mammography CDR). Organized mammography exists 40-74; sensitivity drops in dense breasts (modeled 0.68 vs 0.85 fatty), where MRI adds yield.

High-grade CIN2+ screen-detectable precursor + occult invasive (invasive now rare due to screening). Primary HPV testing preferred. Detected finding is the treatable precursor, so action is largely PREVENTION of invasive cancer.

Modeled prevalence is the clinically-significant (high-grade) subset ~8/1000; raw PSA-elevation detection is ~30-40/1000 men but MOST indolent. USPSTF C (shared decision 55-69). Large overdiagnosis; net QALY small and gated on confirmatory workup + active surveillance for low-grade. prostate_mri (mpMRI) to triage PSA-positives is a pricing_gap that would mitigate the overdiagnosis debit.

BRCA1/2 carrier ~1/400 general pop (~1/40 Ashkenazi). Most carriers unidentified absent family-history flag (~50% lack qualifying FH). Enables RRSO, RRM, intensified MRI surveillance, chemoprevention. hereditary_cancer_panel ($250) dominates brca_full ($2028) on cost across all hereditary findings.

MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2/EPCAM carrier ~1/279. Most carriers unidentified pre-cancer; enables 1-2yr colonoscopy, aspirin, gyn risk-reduction. Single-gene lynch_* codes are gene-dependent partial detectors; the panel detects all genes.
Biallelic MUTYH-associated polyposis ~1/10,000; autosomal recessive, CRC risk 28x / lifetime 40-90%. Monoallelic effect modest -- only biallelic modeled as actionable finding.
Germline TP53 ~1/5,000-20,000; lifetime cancer risk 70-100%. Enables whole-body MRI + breast MRI Toronto-protocol surveillance. Surveillance evidence moderate (observational SIGNIFY/SWEP53).

PALB2, CDH1, STK11/Peutz-Jeghers, APC/FAP, RET/MEN2, VHL, BMPR1A/SMAD4 etc. aggregated high-penetrance actionable syndromes, each individually rare. Aggregated so the panel gets credit beyond BRCA/Lynch/MUTYH/TP53. Actions syndrome-specific surveillance/risk-reducing surgery; evidence moderate.

THIN EVIDENCE -- weak. Any preclinical signal-detectable cancer NOT covered by single-site screens (pancreas, ovary, liver, esophagus, head/neck) caught by MCED stage-shift. NHS-Galleri missed primary endpoint; stage-IV reduction shown but no mortality RCT. Value is stage-shift on otherwise-unscreened lethal cancers; wide uncertainty (could be ~0). galleri/wbmri carry no CPT in the catalog.

LIFESTYLE-MODIFIABLE cancer-incidence risk (the ~18% of cancers attributable to excess weight, inactivity, and poor diet -- colorectal, postmenopausal breast, endometrial). Distinct from the occult_* findings, which are SCREENING-DETECTED prevalent disease: this is PRIMARY PREVENTION of incidence via behavior change. Continuous, graded by cancer_incidence_10yr; threshold 0.05. Detected at intake (BMI + activity + smoking), all $0/low-cost, so this finding FALSE-ZEROS against the present catalog until an intake screen is added. base_catch 0.10 because modifiable cancer prevention is rarely framed or acted on as a discrete intervention. Enables the cancer slices of the physical_activity and mediterranean_diet bundles; observational effect sizes -> moderate/weak.

Latent 20-yr dementia risk above treat-threshold 0.12 (~top quartile genetic+age); delta scales with risk. RISK PREDICTOR ONLY (firewall) -- APOE/PRS never carry benefit; benefit accrues only via the lifestyle/vascular actions. base_catch low: standard care does not act on presymptomatic genetic dementia risk. ad_prs (dementia-specific PRS, distinct from cad_prs) is a pricing_gap; apoe_genotype is present; WES/WGS can derive APOE+PRS.

Latent vascular contribution to dementia (midlife hypertension + LDL + smoking + diabetes); large overlap with cvd/metabolic findings. This is where the strong-evidence actions live (intensive BP lowering: MCI HR 0.81). BP/lipids often managed for CVD but rarely framed/intensified for dementia. office_bp/ambulatory_bp are pricing_gaps; lipid_panel is present.

B12 deficiency / hypothyroidism / folate deficiency contributing to or mimicking cognitive impairment; actionable carriage modeled ~0.02 (only ~0.6% of dementia fully reverses). Cheap, high-value-per-case, partially reversible. B12/TSH commonly on panels but not always acted on for cognition. NOTE: homocysteine has a proven action HERE (deficiency workup) so it is admitted as a supportive detector for this finding only.

Amyloid-positive preclinical/early AD pathology ~0.05 in an older screened slice. Gates the only disease-modifying action (anti-amyloid mAbs), but evidence for ASYMPTOMATIC screening is WEAK and the action is high-harm/high-cost (ARIA). BOTH detectors (plasma_ptau217, amyloid_pet) are pricing_gaps -- this finding false-zeros without them; no present-catalog fallback exists.

Untreated moderate+ hearing loss in higher-risk older adults ~0.10 in target age band; a Lancet-Commission top modifiable factor. Strong, cheap-ish action (hearing aids; ACHIEVE: 48% slower decline in high-risk subgroup). audiometry is the sole detector and a pricing_gap -- this finding false-zeros without it; no present-catalog fallback.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Documented prior MI / ischemic stroke / TIA / symptomatic PAD / >=50% angiographic CAD (clinically established ASCVD)
findings.note: Secondary-prevention population defined mainly by documented event history; intensification (high-intensity statin, antiplatelet, semaglutide) is the gap, not detection.
Paroxysmal AF is intermittent: single ECG misses much of it; extended ambulatory/wearable monitoring is the high-yield detector enabling CHA2DS2-VASc-stratified anticoagulation.
Echo quantifies LVEF and gates the four GDMT pillars plus ICD/CRT at LVEF<=35%; NT-proBNP flags it but does not measure EF.
HFpEF/HFmrEF is a diagnosis of exclusion combining preserved EF, diastolic dysfunction on echo, and raised peptides; frequently undiagnosed. Only SGLT2i has positive-trial benefit.
Murmur on auscultation triggers echo confirmation; CT sizes the annulus for TAVR. Untreated severe symptomatic AS carries ~50% 2-year mortality, so AVR is high-value.
Time-critical: endarterectomy benefit is greatest within 2 weeks of the index event. Carotid duplex is first-line; CTA confirms before surgery.
CCTA identifies high-risk anatomy non-invasively; invasive angiography is the definitive map. Gates CABG in the subset where revascularization beats medical therapy on survival.
Only actionable after a cryptogenic stroke workup excludes other causes; TEE with agitated-saline bubble study confirms a high-risk shunt that PFO closure addresses.
ABI is the cheap first-line confirmatory test; PAD is frequently underdiagnosed. Detection enables dual-pathway inhibition (rivaroxaban+aspirin) and intensified secondary prevention.
Silent until rupture; one-time screening ultrasound has a direct screen-to-mortality RCT (MASS). USPSTF Grade B in ever-smoking men 65-74, but real-world uptake is low.

Active H. pylori is a modifiable gastric-cancer lever; eradication reduces incidence. UBT/stool antigen are the accurate noninvasive detectors; serology cheap but cannot distinguish past from active; EGD biopsy is gold standard but invasive. base_catch modest as testing is symptom-driven, not preventive.

Chronic HBV/HCV and cirrhosis are the dominant modifiable HCC drivers; HBsAg and anti-HCV/HCV-RNA are the serologic detectors, FibroScan stages fibrosis/cirrhosis, abdominal US is the HCC-surveillance modality. USPSTF screens HBV/HCV once in adults so base_catch moderate, but staging and surveillance under-delivered.

Finding is susceptibility to oncogenic HPV-driven cancers in the unvaccinated, hosting catch-up vaccination (cervical, anal, oropharyngeal). Detected chiefly by vaccination-history at intake ($0 gap); hr-HPV DNA detects active oncogenic infection but is not a clean susceptibility marker. Upstream of occult_cervical precursor treatment.

Anal HSIL in high-risk persons is a treatable anal-cancer precursor (ANCHOR trial). Anal cytology +/- hr-HPV triages to high-resolution anoscopy with biopsy (the confirmatory detector). Rarely screened outside specialized HIV/colorectal clinics, so base_catch low.

Dysplastic Barrett's (LGD/HGD) is a treatable esophageal-adenocarcinoma precursor enabling RFA ablation (AIM Dysplasia, SURF). Upper endoscopy with 4-quadrant biopsy is the detector; nonendoscopic cell-collection sponge (Cytosponge/EsoGuard) is an emerging cheaper triage. Screening is symptom/GERD-driven so base_catch modest.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: 5-yr Gail/Tyrer-Cuzick risk >=1.67% (or atypical hyperplasia/LCIS, or dense breasts) without BRCA indication
findings.note: Elevated breast-cancer risk by validated model, atypical hyperplasia/LCIS, or dense breasts WITHOUT a BRCA indication; hosts SERM and aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention. Detected via risk-model scoring at intake plus mammographic density; atypia/LCIS confirmed on biopsy pathology. Chemoprevention is markedly under-offered so base_catch low.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Average/elevated sporadic 10-yr CRC risk (e.g. age >=50 with risk factors), distinct from Lynch; threshold ~ above-average modeled CRC risk
findings.note: Average/elevated sporadic CRC risk amenable to aspirin chemoprevention (distinct from Lynch). Risk estimated from age/family-history/lifestyle at intake; net benefit modest after GI-bleed harm, so a weak continuous finding. Aspirin chemoprevention rarely framed as a discrete preventive action, so base_catch low.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: BMI >=35 (class II+) with adiposity-driven cancer risk; bariatric-eligible band
findings.note: Adiposity-driven risk of obesity-associated cancers (endometrial, postmenopausal breast, colorectal, esophageal, others); lever is bariatric/metabolic surgery weight loss. BMI is the free primary detector; this is the cancer-outcome slice, distinct from the CV/diabetes credit booked under metabolic_surgery. base_catch modest as surgery is rarely framed for cancer prevention.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: UACR >=30 mg/g (or >=300 nephrotic-range/biopsy-confirmed glomerular) in NON-diabetic CKD
findings.note: Non-diabetic proteinuric/glomerular CKD (IgAN, FSGS, membranous, lupus, hypertensive). UACR/microalbumin detect the proteinuria axis; serology + biopsy assign the glomerular subtype that gates disease-specific drugs (sparsentan, TR-budesonide, rituximab, lupus immunotherapy). Mostly eGFR-slope/proteinuria-remission surrogate evidence -> weak-moderate; overlaps ckd_albuminuric (non-diabetic subset).

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Non-dialysis CKD (eGFR <60 or UACR >=30) with elevated ASCVD risk; ZERO once dialysis-dependent (4D/AURORA null)
findings.note: CKD-attributable ASCVD risk in non-dialysis CKD (SHARP population) enabling statin+ezetimibe. CRITICAL: null benefit in maintenance dialysis -- finding zeroes out once dialysis-dependent. eGFR/UACR establish non-dialysis CKD stratum; lipid_panel grades the lipid-driven component. Overlaps cvd_risk_ascvd -- best-net, no double-count of the statin delta.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: eGFR <15 mL/min/1.73m2 (KDIGO G5) or on kidney replacement therapy
findings.note: Established kidney failure / ESRD (eGFR<15 or on KRT). Gates the modality decision -- pre-emptive/early transplant vs dialysis, the largest single survival lever (act_kidney_transplant). At this stage eGFR is almost always measured and dialysis status is overt, so base_catch is high; the gap is referral-to-transplant, not detection.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: BMI >=30 + LVEF >40% with symptomatic HF (elevated NT-proBNP or invasively confirmed filling pressures)
findings.note: Obesity-related HFpEF phenotype; causal lever is adiposity (metabolic), enables incretin_hfpef_obesity + metabolic slice of sglt2i_hfpef. echo confirms preserved EF + diastolic dysfunction, NT-proBNP supports (lower in obese HFpEF -> sensitivity capped), BMI flags the obese subset. Heavy overlap with CVD/HF domain; book the adiposity-driven delta here.

Population-restricted (pregnant). USPSTF Grade B universal screen at 24-28wk is standard care so base_catch high; OGTT confirmatory, 50g glucose-challenge is the standard first-step screen (pricing_gap). Enables gdm_treatment_perinatal/gdm_screen_treat with RCT-proven perinatal-outcome benefit (ACHOIS/MFMU).

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Established diabetes with proliferative DR or diabetic macular edema (sight-threatening) on retinal exam
findings.note: Sight-threatening DR (PDR/DME); real outcome is blindness prevented, not an imaging surrogate. Enables retinopathy_screen_treat (panretinal laser, anti-VEGF). All three detectors are pricing_gaps; autonomous-AI retinal screening (IDx-DR/EyeArt) is FDA-cleared point-of-care; OCT best for DME. Annual dilated exam is guideline standard but under-delivered -> base_catch 0.50.

Presymptomatic (stage 1-2) autoimmune T1D: multiple islet autoantibodies positive. Enables teplizumab_t1d_delay (TN-10, delays stage 3 by ~2-3y). islet_autoantibodies (GAD65/IA-2/ZnT8) is the defining detector (pricing_gap); OGTT stages dysglycemia (stage 2); c_peptide weak adjunct. General-population autoantibody screening has no screen->outcome RCT yet -> base_catch low.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: MVPA <150 min/wk (or low step count, e.g. <5000/day)
findings.note: Cross-cutting VOLUME construct (cvd/metabolic/cancer/neuro) feeding the physical_activity bundle (_rx/_metabolic/_cancer + act_physical_activity_dementia). physical_activity_intake is the $0 intake-screen pricing_gap (Exercise Vital Sign questionnaire); wearable step/MVPA capture is more objective. Activity rarely framed/acted on as a discrete intervention -> base_catch 0.10; finding false-zeros against present catalog until intake screen added.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Dietary-quality adherence score below target (e.g. AHEI/MIND/PREDIMED-Med-diet score in lowest tertile)
findings.note: Cross-cutting (cvd/metabolic/cancer/neuro). Low-quality dietary pattern by adherence score. CV+metabolic slices RCT-backed (PREDIMED); cancer/neuro observational -> weak. Hosts dietary_pattern_med plus component rungs fiber_wholegrain_intake and processed_red_meat_reduction (best-net within bundle, not additive). Sole detector is a $0 intake dietary-quality screener (pricing_gap) -> finding false-zeros against present catalog; base_catch 0.10 (diet rarely scored/acted on systematically).

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: Habitual sodium >~2300 mg/day (or 24h urine Na above target) in BP-sensitive/hypertensive/CKD patients
findings.note: Cross-cutting (cvd/ckd). Habitual sodium above target. SSaSS salt-substitution -> stroke RR 0.86 is a hard-outcome lifestyle RCT but high-baseline population; very-low-intake J-curve tail excluded -> moderate. Folded into lifestyle_bp_lowering (shares BP mediator). Dietary screener is the $0 intake proxy; 24h/spot urine sodium is the objective measure (both pricing_gaps). base_catch 0.10.

Diagnosed MCI/mild dementia due to amyloid-confirmed AD; gates anti-amyloid mAb (CLARITY-AD/TRAILBLAZER-ALZ2). Distinct from preclinical_AD_biomarker (asymptomatic). Standard care catches the symptomatic syndrome moderately but amyloid confirmation gating mAb therapy is under-done.

findings.prevalence_or_threshold: AF present with CHA2DS2-VASc >=2 (men) / >=3 (women); AF prevalence ~2-4% of adults, rising with age
findings.note: AF with elevated cardioembolic stroke risk (CHA2DS2-VASc). Captures the cerebrovascular/disability slice of AF anticoagulation (doac_afib_anticoagulation); credit stroke endpoint once across CVD/neuro. resting_ecg catches sustained AF; prolonged wearable/patch monitoring catches paroxysmal AF that a single ECG misses.

Significant asymptomatic ICA stenosis >=70% gating act_cea_asymptomatic_carotid. Net benefit modest in the modern medical-therapy era and erased by perioperative risk >~3%; rarely sought absent a bruit, so base_catch low. carotid_duplex is the first-line detector.

Diagnosed Parkinson disease with motor symptoms/complications; gates act_dbs_parkinson (EARLYSTIM) and act_exercise_parkinson (SPARX). Diagnosis is clinical (movement-disorder exam); DaTscan resolves PD vs essential tremor / drug-induced parkinsonism. Symptomatic PD is usually clinically recognized, so base_catch is moderate-high; the gap is timely DBS referral and structured exercise prescription. Prodromal/presymptomatic detectors remain research-stage.

Diagnosed relapsing (or active SPMS) MS gating act_ms_dmt (reduces confirmed EDSS progression and relapses). Detection is MRI + McDonald criteria, not a population screen; gadolinium-enhancing/new T2 lesions define disease activity. Symptomatic MS is usually diagnosed, so the lever is early high-efficacy DMT initiation rather than detection.

Idiopathic NPH (gait disturbance + ventriculomegaly) -- a treatable gait-predominant mimic in the cognitive-impairment workup, gating act_nph_shunt (PENS NEJM 2025: gait/falls benefit, cognition unchanged). brain_mri shows ventriculomegaly/DESH; a high-volume-tap (CSF tap test) predicts shunt response. Frequently missed/attributed to aging, so base_catch is low.

Untreated correctable vision impairment, chiefly operable cataract (Lancet-2024 modifiable dementia factor); sensory analogue of hearing_loss_actionable. Gates act_cataract_surgery_dementia -- observational only, so the dementia-benefit evidence is weak. visual_acuity_screen is the cheap first-line detector; full ophthalmologic exam confirms operable cause.

pricing_gaps.label: Office blood pressure measurement
pricing_gaps.note: No discrete CPT for vitals; assume captured at intake at zero marginal cost (matches toy catalog office_bp=$0). Required detector for the BP-driven CVD/renal/dementia components.

pricing_gaps.label: Ambulatory BP monitoring (masked/white-coat HTN)
pricing_gaps.note: CPT 93784/93786/93790. Detects masked hypertension office BP misses; absent from catalog.

pricing_gaps.label: Familial hypercholesterolemia targeted panel (LDLR/APOB/PCSK9/LDLRAP1)
pricing_gaps.note: Cheap targeted cascade-enabling FH detector. Catalog has WES/WGS (incidentally cover FH) + CAD PRS (polygenic, misses monogenic) + hereditary CANCER panel (wrong genes), but no dedicated FH panel.

pricing_gaps.label: Resting 12-lead ECG
pricing_gaps.note: First-line phenotypic detector for long-QT and other inherited arrhythmias; absent from catalog. WES/WGS substitute at high cost.

pricing_gaps.label: Urinalysis with microscopy (hematuria/qualitative proteinuria)
pricing_gaps.note: CPT 81001/81003. Needed for hematuria triage (Alport); only quantitative microalbumin/UACR are in the catalog.

pricing_gaps.label: Dedicated renal/retroperitoneal ultrasound
pricing_gaps.note: CPT 76770 complete / 76775 limited. Currently proxied by abdominal_us (76700, $114), acceptable but not kidney-specific for ADPKD cyst burden.

pricing_gaps.label: Targeted APOL1 G1/G2 genotyping
pricing_gaps.note: Cheap focused 2-variant assay. Only WES/WGS in catalog can detect APOL1, vastly over-priced for this test.

pricing_gaps.label: Nephropathy/monogenic-kidney NGS panel (PKD1/2, COL4A3/4/5, UMOD, NPHS1/2, complement)
pricing_gaps.note: Catalog has only generic carrier/exome/genome tests; no kidney-specific panel, so monogenic kidney findings resolve only via wes_proband/wgs_proband ($4780-$5031).

pricing_gaps.label: MODY / monogenic-diabetes panel (HNF1A, HNF4A, GCK, HNF1B, KCNJ11, ABCC8, INS; +lipodystrophy LMNA/PPARG)
pricing_gaps.note: Catalog has only broad WES/WGS + cancer panel; no diabetes monogenic panel, overstating cost of catching MODY/NDM/lipodystrophy.

pricing_gaps.label: Islet autoantibodies (GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8)
pricing_gaps.note: Needed to distinguish T1/LADA from T2/MODY before genetic testing; not in catalog. Without it, T1-vs-T2-vs-MODY triage relies only on c_peptide.

pricing_gaps.label: BMI / waist circumference (exam measure)
pricing_gaps.note: Free patient-reported/exam measure, not a paid catalog test; modeled as a zero-cost line matching the office_bp convention so the obesity finding has a clean primary detector.

pricing_gaps.label: Tobacco-use status assessment (intake history)
pricing_gaps.note: Smoking status captured at intake at zero marginal cost (matches the office_bp/anthropometric_bmi convention). cotinine (CPT 82505, now in the catalog) is the present objective-confirmation detector, so tobacco_use_active does NOT false-zero.

pricing_gaps.label: AUDIT-C hazardous-alcohol-use screen (intake questionnaire)
pricing_gaps.note: CPT 99408 (alcohol/substance screening + brief intervention), $0 marginal as an intake questionnaire. Sole detector for excess_alcohol_use, which therefore false-zeros against the present catalog (like bp_driven_risk) until an intake screen is catalogued.

pricing_gaps.label: Physical-activity / fitness assessment (intake; e.g. Exercise Vital Sign, wearable)
pricing_gaps.note: Activity level / cardiorespiratory fitness captured at intake at zero marginal cost (Exercise Vital Sign questionnaire or wearable), matching the office_bp/anthropometric_bmi convention. Detector for the lifestyle-modifiable cancer-incidence and (conceptually) the physical_activity bundle's exposure.

pricing_gaps.label: Multiparametric prostate MRI (mpMRI)
pricing_gaps.note: Modern prostate pathway uses mpMRI to triage PSA-positives and cut overdiagnosis; absent from catalog. Its absence means occult_prostate's overdiagnosis debit cannot be mitigated by the modeled panel.

pricing_gaps.label: Dementia/Alzheimer polygenic risk score (AD-PRS)
pricing_gaps.note: Dementia-specific PRS distinct from cad_prs (cardiovascular); needed for genetic dementia risk beyond APOE. Derivable from WES/WGS but no standalone SKU.

pricing_gaps.label: Plasma p-tau217 / p-tau217:Abeta42 ratio
pricing_gaps.note: Only scalable detector for preclinical AD; AUC 0.95-0.96 vs amyloid PET. Not in catalog -- preclinical_AD_biomarker false-zeros without it.

pricing_gaps.label: Amyloid PET (or CSF Abeta42/40 + p-tau via LP)
pricing_gaps.note: Confirmatory detector gating anti-amyloid mAbs; absent from catalog.

pricing_gaps.label: Audiometry / pure-tone hearing screen
pricing_gaps.note: CPT 92557/92551, cheap (~$30-80). Sole detector for hearing_loss_actionable; not in catalog -- finding false-zeros without it.

pricing_gaps.label: Extended ambulatory ECG / wearable patch monitor (7-14 day)
Gated cardiac/aortic CT for TAVR planning (annulus sizing)
Transesophageal echo with agitated-saline bubble study
Transcranial Doppler with bubble study (right-to-left shunt)
Hepatitis C Antibody + reflex HCV RNA
Breast cancer risk model scoring (Gail/Tyrer-Cuzick, intake)
Breast core-needle biopsy with pathology (atypia/LCIS confirmation)
Colorectal cancer risk model scoring (intake questionnaire)
Glomerular workup serology panel (ANA/anti-dsDNA, ANCA, anti-PLA2R, complement C3/C4, SPEP/serum free light chains, hepatitis serologies)
Autonomous AI Diabetic Retinopathy Screening (point-of-care, FDA-cleared)
Submaximal Exercise / Cardiorespiratory Fitness Assessment (Exercise Vital Sign, e.g. step or treadmill submaximal test)
Wearable-derived fitness / activity metrics (estimated VO2max, steps, MVPA)
Dietary-quality / pattern adherence screener (intake questionnaire; AHEI/MIND/Med-diet)
Urine Sodium (24-hour or spot, for habitual intake estimation)
CSF Abeta42/40 ratio + p-tau (lumbar puncture biomarker panel)
Ambulatory / wearable single-lead ECG patch (paroxysmal AF detection)
DaTscan (ioflupane I-123 SPECT, striatal dopamine transporter imaging)
Brain + cervical spine MRI with and without contrast (MS protocol)
CSF oligoclonal bands + IgG index (lumbar puncture)
High-volume CSF tap test / lumbar drain trial (NPH shunt-response prediction)
